title: Account Retention
subtitle: GDPR-friendly automatic deletion of inactive accounts. Configure thresholds and exemptions below.
general.notice: Keep "Dry-run" enabled during initial setup. In dry-run the plugin counts and logs but does not send emails (unless you enable it separately) and does not delete accounts.
field.enabled.help: Disabling stops the cron. Scheduled actions will not be performed.
field.dryRun.help: Runs the full pipeline without deleting accounts. Audit records what WOULD happen. Recommended for first runs.
field.emailsEnabledInDryRun.help: Off by default. Enable only when you want to test templates against real addresses.
field.cronHour.help: UTC hour at which the daily scan runs. Default 03:00 UTC.
field.inactivityDays.help: Account is deleted after this many days without login. Default 365.
field.warningDays.help: E.g. "30,7" = send warnings 30 and 7 days before deletion. Smallest value becomes the "final warning".
field.keepaliveTokenTtlDays.help: How long the keep-alive token in the email stays valid. Should be ≥ the largest warning day.
field.gracePeriodDaysAfterInstall.help: Safety net: for the first N days after install no destructive actions run. Default 14.
field.auditRetentionDays.help: How long to keep audit entries. Suggested ≥ 365 for GDPR evidence. Default 1095 (3 years).
field.exemptGroups.help: Comma-separated group names. Defaults to administrators and global moderators.
field.exemptUids.help: Comma-separated UIDs. Service accounts, bots, backup accounts.
field.includeBanned.help: Default yes — from a GDPR standpoint a banned account is still inactive. Disable if you want to keep the ban as a permanent record.
field.includeNeverLoggedIn: Delete accounts that never logged in
field.includeNeverLoggedIn.help: Counted from registration date when no login exists.
pending.notice: List of users that would be touched on the next run. This is a preview — nothing happens here automatically.
save.help: Saving immediately reloads the plugin configuration.
